text: Meeting deadlines can be challenging, but good time management helps. Start by breaking large tasks into smaller steps with their own mini-deadlines. Prioritization is key—focus on the most important or urgent tasks first. If a project feels overwhelming, consider scope management: clarify what is truly required and avoid adding extra work. Finally, communication matters. If you sense you might miss a deadline, inform your manager or team early. They may adjust expectations or offer support. By planning ahead, staying focused, and keeping others informed, you can handle deadlines with less stress and more success.
